items.title: S&P 500, Dow, Nasdaq hit record closes as earnings fuel rally
items.content: S&P 500 rose 0.6% to 7,563.63, Dow gained 24.69 pts to 50,668.97, Nasdaq climbed 242.74 pts to 26,917.47 — all record closes. Supported by strong earnings from Dollar Tree, Snowflake, Hormel Foods and a tentative Iran ceasefire extension.

items.title: Hang Seng Index drops 1.27% to 25,006 on trade tensions
items.content: HSI closed at 25,006.16, lowest since early April. Morning session dropped 2.32% to 24,740.39. Pressure from US-China trade tensions, elevated global interest rates, and Chinese property sector concerns.

items.title: April PCE inflation rises 3.8% YoY, highest since May 2023
items.content: Headline PCE 3.8% YoY (slightly below 3.9% forecast, up from 3.5% in March). Core PCE 3.3% YoY, in line. Monthly headline +0.4%. Gasoline +5.5% spike drove increase. Consumer spending +0.5% nominal, but real PCE +0.1% only. Saving rate fell to 2.6%.

items.title: US-Iran clashes resume in Strait of Hormuz despite ceasefire talks
items.content: Iran fired drones at 4 ships attempting to cross strait without authorization. US struck ground control station in Bandar Abbas. Both sides accused each other of violations. Iran launched ballistic missile toward Kuwait (intercepted). Tentative 60-day ceasefire extension proposed but not yet approved by Trump.

items.title: China chip stocks rally on AI optimism and Huawei 1.4nm breakthrough
items.content: Chinese semiconductor and AI stocks rose. Huawei announced potential 1.4nm 'LogicFolding' tech by 2031. Baidu aims to deliver ~500,000 AI accelerators in 2026, more than tripling 2025 output. CXMT memory chip revenue expanded significantly. SMIC, Huawei central to self-sufficiency push.

items.title: Best Buy Q1 FY27 beats estimates, shares jump 10%+
items.content: Revenue $8.936B vs est. $8.82B (+1.9% YoY). Comparable sales +2.0% (best in 4 years). Adj. EPS $1.28 vs est. EPS growth +38% to $1.31. Gaming, computing, mobile drove growth. CEO Corie Barry to step down, Jason Bonfig CEO Nov 1.

items.title: Snowflake Q1 FY27 blockbuster: revenue $1.39B, raises guidance
items.content: Total revenue $1.39B (+33.5% YoY), product revenue $1.33B (+34%). Adj. EPS $0.39 vs est. $0.32. 754 Forbes G2000 customers. NRR expanded to 126%. Announced $6B AWS partnership and Natoma acquisition. Raised full-year product revenue and margin guidance.

items.title: Dollar Tree Q1 beats: EPS $1.74, raises full-year guidance
items.content: Net sales $5B (+7.2% YoY). Comparable sales +3.5%, ticket size +4.5%. Adj. EPS $1.74 vs est. $1.53 (+38% YoY). Margin expanded 120bps. Raised FY26 EPS guidance to $6.70-$7.10. Plans 400 new stores, ~75 closures.

items.title: PCE inflation reinforces cautious Fed stance under Chair Warsh
items.content: April PCE data released as new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh weighs next rate moves. Iran conflict driving energy price uncertainty. Market expects rates to remain on hold with inflation running above 3% target. Real spending barely positive suggests consumer fatigue.

items.title: Chinese AI chip makers rally as self-sufficiency narrative strengthens
items.content: SMIC, Huawei chip supply chain stocks rally on policy support and restricted US tech access. Huawei's 'LogicFolding' tech could meaningfully narrow gap with global leaders. Baidu tripling AI accelerator output signals strong domestic demand shift away from Nvidia.

items.title: US consumer saving rate drops to 2.6% as inflation eats into incomes
items.content: Personal saving rate fell from elevated levels to 2.6% in April, suggesting households drawing on savings to cover higher costs. Gasoline +5.5%, housing and utilities up. Real PCE growth of just 0.1% signals consumer spending momentum may be fading.

items.title: Baidu to triple AI accelerator output to 500,000 units in 2026
items.content: Baidu plans ~500K AI accelerator deliveries in 2026, more than 3x 2025 output. Huawei Ascend chips gaining traction as domestic alternatives to Nvidia. China's AI investment boom driving semiconductor export and import growth simultaneously.
